Additional detail: One of those scenarios have manifested as a real terminate unexpectedly for us, in our custom fork of 4.6.1-stable, as seen in this crash backrace: More specifically it's (in the `4.6` branch) this mutex that's already been destroyed by the time `PagedAllocator` tries to print its leak report: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/5bd79ab53ffe9952f371a10e31c17947cf9a176b/core/object/object.cppL2406-L2408 As far as I can tell there has been some refactoring to the top part of this backtrace in latest `master`, but the `static BinaryMutex` involved is still there, as are the static `Variant` pools and the leak reporting, so this should still be just as much of a problem. This makes the feature unreliable for practical use.
